workflow.name: Discover local business leads and run AI-powered cold email sequences with Google Maps and Gmail
workflow.description:
# Discover local business leads and run automated cold email sequences with Google Maps and Gmail

This workflow is a complete outbound automation system that discovers local businesses, extracts contact emails, generates personalized cold emails using AI, and runs a multi-step follow-up sequence — fully automated.

It is designed for founders, agencies, and consultants who want to generate leads at scale without manual scraping or repetitive email work.

---

## Use cases

- Local service lead generation (marketing agencies, SaaS, consultants)
- B2B prospecting by location and category
- Automated cold outreach campaigns
- Market research and business discovery
- Sales pipeline bootstrapping

---

## How it works

1. Reads ZIP codes and business categories from Google Sheets  
2. Queries Google Maps API for matching businesses  
3. Visits each website and extracts public emails  
4. Stores leads in Google Sheets  
5. Uses AI to generate intro + 2 follow-up emails  
6. Sends emails via Gmail  
7. Automatically schedules follow-ups  
8. Tracks status and prevents duplicates  

Everything runs on schedules and updates itself.

---

## How to use

1. Create a Google Sheet with:
   - `Zips` tab (ZIP codes)
   - `Google Maps Categories` tab
   - `Results` tab

2. Add:
   - Google Maps API key
   - Gmail account
   - Google Sheets credentials

3. Replace placeholders:
   - Company name
   - Logo URL
   - CTA text

4. Enable schedule triggers.

That’s it. The system runs continuously.

---

## Requirements

- Google Maps API key  
- Google account (Sheets + Gmail)  
- Gemini / OpenAI API key  
- Public company logo URL  

No paid scraping tools required.

---

## Good to know

- Uses exponential backoff to handle API limits safely  
- Prevents duplicate leads using place_id  
- Tracks email status and send dates  
- Follow-ups only send if intro was delivered  
- All data stays in your Google Sheets  

---

## Customising this workflow

You can easily:

- Change email copy style in the AI prompt  
- Adjust follow-up delays  
- Add more follow-up steps  
- Replace Gmail with Outlook or SMTP  
- Push leads into CRM instead of Sheets  

This workflow is modular and extensible.

---

## What this n8n template demonstrates

- Real-world lead generation automation  
- Web scraping with fallback logic  
- AI-powered copy generation  
- Stateful workflow design  
- Multi-trigger scheduling  
- Error handling and retries  
- Production-grade outbound system  

This is not a demo — it’s a deployable business workflow.

---

## Need a custom setup?

If you want a similar system built for your business (custom data sources, CRM integration, WhatsApp bots, booking systems, dashboards, or private deployments), feel free to reach out at dinakars2003@gmail.com

values.content:
=Generate a JSON object with 3 fields: - intro_mail - follow_up_mail_1 - follow_up_mail_2 Each field must contain a complete cold outreach email content. Inputs: - Recipient title: {{ $json.title }} - Business type: {{ $json.type }} - Google Maps categories: {{ $json.types }} Our company: YOUR COMPANY NAME Our services: - Digital marketing - SEO - Website development - Automation (n8n) - AI-powered workflows - Business process optimization Rules for EACH email: - Start with a friendly greeting using the business type or title (example: "Hi there," or "Hi {{ $json.title }},"). - DO NOT include any signature or closing. - DO NOT repeat the business name more than once. - Use

for line breaks. - No other HTML. - 4 to 5 short sentences. - Friendly, professional, human tone. - Mention automation or AI at least once. - End with a soft value-based statement (not a meeting request). Additional sequencing rules: - intro_mail: general introduction and value. - follow_up_mail_1: gentle nudge, assume they saw the first email. - follow_up_mail_2: final follow-up, slightly more direct but still polite. Output format: Return ONLY valid JSON. No explanations. No extra text.

parameters.content:
## How it works

This workflow automatically finds local business leads from Google Maps, extracts contact emails from their websites, generates personalized cold emails using AI, and sends a complete 3-step outreach sequence (intro + 2 follow-ups).

It runs on a schedule, processes new locations by ZIP code and business category, stores everything in Google Sheets, and tracks which emails were sent and when. Follow-ups are triggered automatically based on dates, so no manual work is needed after setup.

The system includes retry logic, rate-limit handling, and status tracking to make it safe for long-running automation.

## Setup steps

1. Create a Google Sheet with tabs for ZIP codes, categories, and results or make a copy of [GSheets Link](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1BRPF4IHoxtAIE5gBQMNP_qIux_XNDXTnmIEHYcl5kEk/edit?usp=sharing)
2. Add your Google Maps API key.
3. Connect Google Sheets and Gmail credentials.
4. Replace placeholders (company name, logo, CTA).
5. Enable schedule triggers.

Once enabled, the workflow runs fully hands-free.

## How to run

1. Go to Google Sheets and open the sheet named **Zips**.  
   Add the locations you want to scrape, for example:  
   - 600001  
   - Anna Nagar, Chennai, Tamil Nadu  
   You can add multiple rows.

2. Open the sheet named **Google Maps Categories**.  
   Add business categories you want to target, for example:  
   - Veg restaurants  
   - Dental clinics  
   - Software companies  

3. Run the first part of the workflow to generate leads.

4. After publishing the workflow, the email writer runs automatically every minute.  
   It fills:
   - Intro mail  
   - Follow-up mail 1  
   - Follow-up mail 2  

   Only for rows where **Intro mail is empty**.

5. The intro mail sender runs when:
   - Intro mail is not empty  
   - Email status is empty  

   It sends the intro email immediately.

6. Follow-up logic:
   - Follow-up 1 is sent 7 days after the intro mail.  
   - Follow-up 2 is sent 11 days after the intro mail.  

   The workflow stores message IDs to send follow-ups as replies.

7. Follow-up workflows run every day at **8 AM**.  
   They check if today is the follow-up date and send emails only when due.

## Lead discovery

Reads ZIP codes and business categories from Google Sheets, then queries Google Maps to find matching local businesses.

## Website scraping

Visits each business website and extracts public email addresses from the page content.

## Lead storage
Saves cleaned lead data (name, email, phone, rating, website) into the Results sheet.

## AI email generation
Generates personalized intro and follow-up emails using business data and categories.

## Lead discovery

Reads ZIP codes and business categories from Google Sheets, then queries Google Maps to find matching local businesses.

## Intro send
Sends first outreach email and stores message ID.

## Follow up 1
Replies to intro email after 7 days.

## Follow up 2
Final reply after 11 days.

## Rate limit handling
Prevents Google Sheets API limits using
exponential backoff and retries.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.

data.details: The Code node allows you to execute JavaScript in your workflow.

The Filter node can be used to filter items based on a condition. If the condition is met, the item will be passed on to the next node. If the condition is not met, the item will be omitted. Conditions can be combined together by AND(meet all conditions), or OR(meet at least one condition).
